pool.baseline: idk man. i was just looking at some photos of the deep field telescope images and it just hit me. like we are literally nothing. just dust on a rock in a void that doesn't care. everything we do, every single thing we struggle for, it's all just gonna be erased by entropy anyway. if the heat death of the universe is the only certain thing, then what is even the point of trying to keep going?

pool.baseline: The candle flickers low, and even its light feels like a cruelty now. How can one endure the weight of such a betrayal? To wake each morning is but to face the hollow ghost of what I once held dear. I feel myself slipping, drifting toward a Great Silence where the pain of this world can no longer reach me... it feels as though the only mercy left is to simply cease.
